Super fun experience and easy to do in a few hours. Lots of great memorabilia, well displayed and not too crowded.

I've visited this museum a few times since it opened.  My first visit was one of excitement and wonder.  The museum on the corner of Wilshire and Fairfax used to be a May Company Department Store and retains its Streamline Moderne architecture.  The connected globe-shaped theatre and observation deck that looks like the Death Star from Star Wars is new construction.  The museum is just steps away from the LACMA campus, which just opened its new huge David Geffen Galleries.  The museum doesn't have its own parking, but you can use LACMA's garage or the one at the Petersen Automotive Museum across Wilshire (it's more $ vs LACMA's).  There's some street parking, but watch for time and resident restrictions.

The museum has both permanent and temporary exhibitions.  The permanent ones seem to be the Oscars one, where many Oscars are on display in a circular room; the next room has more items on display, plus clips of various acceptance speeches.  There's an Oscar Experience where you pay to hold an Oscar and give an acceptance speech.  There's also a paid experience to sit behind the desk from The Godfather.  

The temporary exhibit I came to see this time was Jaws, the Spielberg film celebrating its 50th anniversary.  It was nice, but a model of Bruce the shark hangs permanently in the lobby.  The last exhibit I came to see was called Joh Waters: Pope of Trash.  That was a more in-depth exhibit.  

The rooftop observation deck offers decent views looking north.  You can see the Hollywood sign.  On the ground level is a very good gift shop and a fine restaurant/cafe called Fanny's  where you can to a quick bite in the cafe or a kind of  fancy meal in the restaurant proper, which has a nice menu and decor.

Worth a visit.

We finally took some time and headed to L.A to go to the museum.  We were there on a Thursday, the Thursday before the Oscars so we expected a crowd.  It was busy, but not packed. The museum is large and open so you don't feel closed in.  We made our way across all the floors and exhibits, and found it to be really cool.  They do change the exhibits, but while we visited there was a Barbie exhibit, Jaws exhibit, a room on Bong Joon Ha and movies.  They have costumes, Oscar statues, and so much information.  We had so much fun exploring, reading, watching clips of Oscar highlights, and movies.  They have scripts, and more.  We spent about 3.5 to 4 hours and had so much fun.

Once you get to the top floor take the time to go our on the terrace, the views over Hollywood are stunning.

We parked in the parking lot for the Peterson Auto Museum which is recommended and it worked out perfectly.  It was just a short walk to the museum. It isn't cheap, but we didn't see any street parking, so we grabbed a spot in the lot.
